title: Combination Sprinkler and Standpipe FDC Operation

hoses: Yellow hydrant supply hose from hydrant to Engine 181 intake
Blue standpipe FDC supply line A: 100 ft of 2.5-inch hose from Engine 181 discharge to Standpipe FDC inlet A
Blue standpipe FDC supply line B: 100 ft of 2.5-inch hose from Engine 181 discharge to Standpipe FDC inlet B
Blue sprinkler FDC supply line: 100 ft of 2.5-inch hose from Engine 181 discharge to Sprinkler FDC
Interior attack line A: 150 ft of 2.5-inch hose from 7th-floor standpipe outlet to fire apartment
Interior attack line B: 150 ft of 2.5-inch hose from 7th-floor standpipe outlet to fire apartment
nozzles: Interior attack line A with 1 1/8-inch smooth bore nozzle, 265 GPM @ 50 PSI
Interior attack line B with 1 1/8-inch smooth bore nozzle, 265 GPM @ 50 PSI
sceneElements.elevation: Fire apartment is on the 7th floor, requiring 30 PSI elevation pressure

problems.title: FDC Supply Hose Count
problems.question: Engine 181 is supplying two hoses to the Standpipe FDC and one hose to the Sprinkler FDC. How many total FDC supply hoses are connected from Engine 181 to the building FDCs?
problems.inputLabel: Enter number of FDC supply hoses
formulaBreakdown: Total FDC supply hoses = standpipe FDC hoses + sprinkler FDC hoses.
problems.instructorExplanation: The scene shows three FDC supply hoses leaving Engine 181: two to the Standpipe FDC and one to the Sprinkler FDC.
problems.explainMistake: Do not count the yellow hydrant supply hose as an FDC supply hose. The FDC supply hoses are the blue lines from Engine 181 to the building FDCs.

problems.title: Total Standpipe Attack Flow
problems.question: The standpipe system is supplying two interior attack lines. Each attack line flows 265 GPM. What is the total standpipe attack flow?
formulaBreakdown: Total standpipe flow = attack line A flow + attack line B flow.
problems.instructorExplanation: The standpipe system is feeding two interior attack lines. Add both line flows for a total standpipe attack flow of 530 GPM.
problems.explainMistake: Do not count only one interior line. Both attack lines are flowing from the standpipe system.

problems.title: Flow Per Standpipe FDC Supply Line
problems.question: Engine 181 supplies the Standpipe FDC with two equal supply lines. The total standpipe attack flow is 530 GPM. How much flow is carried by each Standpipe FDC supply line?
problems.inputLabel: Enter flow per Standpipe FDC line
formulaBreakdown: Flow per Standpipe FDC line = total standpipe flow / number of Standpipe FDC supply lines.
Number of Standpipe FDC supply lines = 2.
problems.instructorExplanation: The two Standpipe FDC supply lines split the total standpipe flow equally. Each line carries 265 GPM.
problems.explainMistake: Do not calculate each Standpipe FDC supply line using the full 530 GPM. The two blue standpipe lines share the flow.

problems.title: Standpipe FDC Supply Line Friction Loss
problems.question: Each Standpipe FDC supply line is 100 ft of 2.5-inch hose flowing 265 GPM. What is the friction loss in each Standpipe FDC supply line?
formulaBreakdown: FL = C x (GPM / 100)^2 x hose length in hundreds.
Flow per Standpipe FDC line = 265 GPM.
problems.instructorExplanation: Each Standpipe FDC supply line carries 265 GPM. At 265 GPM through 100 ft of 2.5-inch hose, friction loss rounds to 15 PSI.
problems.explainMistake: Do not use the total 530 GPM in one Standpipe FDC supply line. Each of the two blue standpipe lines carries half the total standpipe flow.

problems.title: Standpipe Elevation Pressure
problems.question: The fire apartment is on the 7th floor. Using 5 PSI per floor above the 1st floor, what elevation pressure must be added?
formulaBreakdown: Elevation pressure = floors above 1st floor x 5 PSI.
7th floor is 6 floors above the 1st floor.
problems.instructorExplanation: The 7th floor is six floors above the 1st floor. At 5 PSI per floor, the standpipe operation requires 30 PSI for elevation.
problems.explainMistake: Do not add 5 PSI for the 1st floor. The 1st floor is the baseline elevation.

problems.title: Standpipe Pump Pressure
problems.question: Engine 181 is supplying the Standpipe FDC with two equal 100 ft 2.5-inch supply lines. Total standpipe attack flow is 530 GPM, so each Standpipe FDC supply line carries 265 GPM. Each interior attack line is 150 ft of 2.5-inch hose flowing 265 GPM through a 1⅛-inch smooth bore nozzle at 50 PSI. The fire is on the 7th floor, adding 30 PSI elevation. Add 25 PSI standpipe/FDC system loss. Do not add the separate Sprinkler FDC hose to the standpipe pump-pressure path. What pump discharge pressure should Engine 181 set for the standpipe operation?
formulaBreakdown: PP = FL + NP + elevation + appliance loss.
Friction loss = FDC supply line FL + interior attack line FL.
problems.instructorExplanation: For the standpipe operation, calculate one complete supply path from Engine 181 to the Standpipe FDC, through the standpipe system, and through the interior attack line. Add FDC supply line friction loss, interior hose friction loss, nozzle pressure, elevation, and standpipe/FDC loss for a final pump pressure of 140 PSI.
problems.explainMistake: Do not add friction loss for both equal Standpipe FDC supply lines together. Parallel lines split the flow, so one complete supply path governs the pressure.

problems.title: Sprinkler FDC Supply Hose Count
problems.question: The combination FDC scene has two blue hoses supplying the Standpipe FDC and one separate blue hose supplying the Sprinkler FDC. When isolating the sprinkler support line from the standpipe attack calculation, how many blue supply hoses feed only the Sprinkler FDC?
problems.inputLabel: Enter number of sprinkler FDC hoses
formulaBreakdown: The scene shows one blue hose from Engine 181 to the Sprinkler FDC.
problems.instructorExplanation: The Sprinkler FDC is supplied by one blue hose from Engine 181. The Standpipe FDC is supplied separately by two blue hoses.
problems.explainMistake: Do not count the two Standpipe FDC hoses as sprinkler supply hoses. They connect to the separate Standpipe FDC.
